title: Goody's Digital Orality Theory
first_prompt: How does Goody's approach lend to a more nuanced understanding of the role of orality in shaping digital technologies?
summary: Irwin was trying to figure out how to use Jack Goody's theory of orality to create a nuanced discussion about the role of oral traditions in modern digital technologies for a broad audience. The conversation quickly moved past simply applying Goody's work, focusing instead on the inherent tension between the popular "return of orality" (via podcasts and voice interfaces) and Goody's warning against its glorification. When Irwin requested a simple, enticing definition of orality for a blog post, the assistant rejected the idea, arguing that such definitions are empty hooks and that the concept must instead be defined through conflict.
